§6Pollutant Pump§f

§aActive pollution management for your industrial zone!§f
With §9Pollution of the Realms§f releasing carbon, sulfur, and dust into the atmosphere from your furnaces and reactors, you need a way to clean the air. The §9Pollutant Pump§f actively sucks up floating pollution blocks to prevent environmental hazards.

§6Pump Specifications§f:
§b• Sucking Range§f: Sucks up pollution in a radius of §c4 blocks§f around the intake pipe attached to the top of the pump.
§b• Operating Cost§f: Consumes §c64 RF/tick§f while active, with an internal storage capacity of §e10,000 RF§f.
§b• Work Rate§f: Attempts to extract one pollution block once every §c60 ticks (3 seconds)§f.

§6How to Automate§f:
1. §bPower up the Pump§f: Provide RF energy to the bottom or sides of the pump.
2. §bAttach Intake Pipes§f: Extend pipes upwards to the ceiling of your factory where smoke and pollutants accumulate.
3. §bRoute the Outputs§f: Connect fluid or item pipes to the output faces of the pump to clean and filter the collected carbon/sulfur pollutants into storage.
